sprint: Selection Action Strip — Small Side Widget + a Real Inline Edit Control
task: direct, verbatim follow-up on the just-shipped Selection Action Strip: "this was suppose to open on side small widget not full size on top. second clicking on pencil just makes right pane active with no edit options... the contextual menu needs to work there only. so if its editable give the editing option in context menu itself." Root-caused: js/contextPanel.js's _renderWorldObjectDisclosure already renders the real quick-edit control (Words textarea/colour swatch/Replace Image button) into #contextPanelRoot the instant a World-owned object is selected, but the strip's own focusEditor binding called _activateTab('card'), toggling a legacy, largely-superseded container that never shows that same content -- showing stale/empty content instead of a real control. Fixed by reparameterizing _appendWorldObjectEditControl to take an explicit container and exporting a new ContextPanel.mountQuickEditControl(container,sceneObj) (scoped to owner==='world' && editable with a recognized .visual.kind, deliberately excluding Story-owned stickers, whose content writes through a different mechanism entirely) so js/selectionActionStrip.js's own small popup mounts the SAME real control inline, rather than a second implementation. The strip is now absolutely positioned within .preview-area (position:relative added there); _positionWidget() measures the real gutter on either side of the canvas at runtime and anchors the widget entirely within whichever side has room (guaranteeing zero overlap regardless of how tall the inline popup grows), falling back to a compact in-flow placement above the canvas only when neither gutter is wide enough. A second, real bug found while verifying: clicking Edit still did nothing at first, traced to .preview-area's own 'click empty space deselects' mousedown listener not excluding the strip itself -- since Edit/Deselect are DOM children of .preview-area too, a mousedown on them bubbled up and silently cleared the very selection Edit was about to show a control for. Fixed by widening that listener's closest() guard to also exclude #selectionActionStrip. Verified via a new 18-assertion Playwright suite: the strip renders genuinely narrow with zero canvas overlap; clicking Edit on an editable World-owned text object shows a real, pre-filled Words textarea whose typed edits write a genuine content override exactly matching ContextPanel's own disclosure, surviving an unrelated refresh() without being rebuilt mid-edit; a locked object hides Edit entirely (Guardrails); an Artwork Place always shows Edit but falls back to a plain hint+link. One pre-existing, already-stale regression script was confirmed via git stash to already fail identically on the unmodified baseline (predates the Traveller Gateway's own skip helper), unrelated to this fix. Full regression across the original 15-assertion Option B suite plus text-resize-grid/commitments/canvas-click/paper-bg-hittest/sync-charm suites all pass unchanged, plus both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2). See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full account.
